---
title: "Statera Training Progress"
subtitle: "Klotzbücher, Valentin — Phase 1–12A (Oct 2024 – Feb 2026)"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from itables import show

df = pd.read_csv("data/training_panel.csv")
df["phase_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["phase_date"])

# Phase labels for axes (ordered)
phase_order = df.drop_duplicates("phase_num").sort_values("phase_num")
phase_labels = phase_order["phase"].tolist()

# Only rows with actual weight data
dfw = df.dropna(subset=["weight_kg"]).copy()
dfw["phase"] = pd.Categorical(dfw["phase"], categories=phase_labels, ordered=True)

# Shared color palette — consistent across all charts
PALETTE = {
    "Beinpresse":              "#e74c3c",  # red
    "Kniebeuge Maschine":      "#c0392b",  # dark red
    "Bankdrücken":             "#2980b9",  # blue
    "Schrägbankdrücken":       "#3498db",  # light blue
    "Rudern":                  "#27ae60",  # green
    "Latzug":                  "#2ecc71",  # light green
    "Kreuzheben KH":           "#8e44ad",  # purple
    "Beinbeugen":              "#e67e22",  # orange
    "Schulterdrücken stehend": "#f39c12",  # yellow-orange
    "Trizepsdrücken Seilzug":  "#1abc9c",  # teal
    "Trizepsdrücken liegend":  "#16a085",  # dark teal
    "Bizepscurls":             "#d35400",  # burnt orange
    "Butterfly reverse":       "#9b59b6",  # lavender purple
    "Hammercurls":             "#e84393",  # pink
    "Kickbacks":               "#636e72",  # grey
}

# Shared clean layout defaults
LAYOUT = dict(
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12),
)
GRID = dict(gridcolor="#eee")

def color_for(name):
    return PALETTE.get(name, "#7f8c8d")
```

## Weight Progression Over Phases

All weekly weights for each exercise across phases. Within each phase the dots spread
across weeks; the connected line follows Week 1 starting weights — the main
indicator of progressive overload.

```{python}
# All weeks, deduplicated per phase/exercise/week (keep first plan occurrence)
wall = dfw.drop_duplicates(
    subset=["phase_num", "exercise_normalized", "week"], keep="first"
).copy()

# Build a continuous x-position: phase_num + fractional offset per week
max_weeks = wall.groupby("phase_num")["week"].transform("max")
wall["x_pos"] = wall["phase_num"] + (wall["week"] - 1) / max_weeks * 0.8

# Tick positions at integer phase numbers
tick_vals = sorted(wall["phase_num"].unique())
tick_text = [phase_labels[i] for i, _ in enumerate(tick_vals)]

fig = go.Figure()

exercises = sorted(wall["exercise_normalized"].unique())
for ex in exercises:
    ex_df = wall[wall.exercise_normalized == ex].sort_values("x_pos")
    c = color_for(ex)

    # All weekly dots (smaller, same color)
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=ex_df["x_pos"],
        y=ex_df["weight_kg"],
        mode="markers",
        marker=dict(size=4, color=c, opacity=0.35),
        name=ex,
        legendgroup=ex,
        showlegend=False,
        hovertemplate=f"{ex}<br>Phase %{{customdata[0]}}, Week %{{customdata[1]}}<br>%{{y}} kg<extra></extra>",
        customdata=ex_df[["phase_num", "week"]].values,
    ))

    # Week 1 line (bold, with markers)
    w1 = ex_df[ex_df.week == 1].sort_values("phase_num")
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=w1["x_pos"],
        y=w1["weight_kg"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        marker=dict(size=7, color=c),
        line=dict(color=c, width=2),
        name=ex,
        legendgroup=ex,
        showlegend=True,
        hovertemplate=f"{ex}<br>Phase %{{customdata[0]}}, Week 1<br>%{{y}} kg<extra></extra>",
        customdata=w1[["phase_num", "week"]].values,
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    **LAYOUT,
    height=600,
    xaxis=dict(tickvals=tick_vals, ticktext=tick_text, title="Phase", **GRID),
    yaxis=dict(title="Weight (kg)", **GRID),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.15, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
    hovermode="closest",
)
fig.show()
```

## Weekly Weight Ramp Within Phases

How weight increases week-to-week within each training block, for every exercise.

```{python}
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

phases_in_order = dfw.drop_duplicates("phase_num").sort_values("phase_num")
phase_nums = phases_in_order["phase_num"].tolist()
phase_names = phases_in_order["phase"].tolist()
n = len(phase_nums)
ncols = 4
nrows = -(-n // ncols)  # ceil division

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=nrows, cols=ncols,
    subplot_titles=phase_names,
    shared_yaxes=False,
    horizontal_spacing=0.05,
    vertical_spacing=0.08,
)

all_exercises = sorted(dfw["exercise_normalized"].unique())
shown_in_legend = set()

for idx, pnum in enumerate(phase_nums):
    row = idx // ncols + 1
    col = idx % ncols + 1
    pdf = dfw[dfw.phase_num == pnum].sort_values("week")

    for ex in all_exercises:
        edf = pdf[pdf.exercise_normalized == ex]
        if edf.empty:
            continue
        # Deduplicate if exercise appears in both plans (keep first)
        edf = edf.drop_duplicates(subset=["week"], keep="first")
        c = color_for(ex)
        show_legend = ex not in shown_in_legend
        if show_legend:
            shown_in_legend.add(ex)

        fig.add_trace(
            go.Scatter(
                x=edf["week"],
                y=edf["weight_kg"],
                mode="lines+markers",
                marker=dict(size=4, color=c),
                line=dict(color=c, width=1.5),
                name=ex,
                legendgroup=ex,
                showlegend=show_legend,
                hovertemplate=f"{ex}<br>Week %{{x}}<br>%{{y}} kg<extra></extra>",
            ),
            row=row, col=col,
        )

# Apply white bg and grid to ALL subplots
fig.update_layout(
    **LAYOUT,
    height=250 * nrows,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.05, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
)
fig.update_xaxes(**GRID, title_text="", dtick=1)
fig.update_yaxes(**GRID, title_text="")
fig.show()
```

## Strength Gains Heatmap

Percentage change in peak (last week) weight relative to the first phase each exercise
appears in. Darker green = bigger gains since baseline.

```{python}
# Use last week of each phase (peak weight) per exercise
wmax = dfw.loc[dfw.groupby(["phase_num", "exercise_normalized"])["week"].idxmax()]
wmax = wmax.drop_duplicates(subset=["phase_num", "exercise_normalized"], keep="first")

heat = wmax.pivot_table(
    index="exercise_normalized", columns="phase", values="weight_kg",
    aggfunc="first",
)
heat = heat.reindex(columns=phase_labels)

# Percentage change from each exercise's first non-NaN value
baseline = heat.apply(lambda row: row.dropna().iloc[0] if row.dropna().any() else np.nan, axis=1)
pct = heat.div(baseline, axis=0).subtract(1).multiply(100)

# Sort by total gain (biggest gainers on top)
pct = pct.loc[pct.max(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=False).index]

# Hover shows both % change and absolute kg
hover_text = []
for ex in pct.index:
    row_hover = []
    for phase in pct.columns:
        p = pct.loc[ex, phase]
        kg = heat.loc[ex, phase]
        if pd.notna(p):
            row_hover.append(f"{ex}<br>{phase}<br>{p:+.0f}% ({kg:.4g} kg)")
        else:
            row_hover.append("")
    hover_text.append(row_hover)

# Cell text: show "+X%" (skip baseline 0%)
cell_text = pct.map(lambda v: f"+{v:.0f}%" if pd.notna(v) and v > 0 else ("0%" if pd.notna(v) else ""))

fig = go.Figure(
    go.Heatmap(
        z=pct.values,
        x=pct.columns.tolist(),
        y=pct.index.tolist(),
        colorscale=[[0, "#f7f7f7"], [0.5, "#a1d99b"], [1, "#006d2c"]],
        colorbar=dict(title="%", ticksuffix="%"),
        hoverongaps=False,
        hovertext=hover_text,
        hovertemplate="%{hovertext}<extra></extra>",
        text=cell_text.values,
        texttemplate="%{text}",
        textfont=dict(size=10),
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    **LAYOUT,
    height=max(400, len(pct) * 35 + 100),
    xaxis=dict(title="Phase", side="bottom"),
    yaxis=dict(title="", autorange="reversed"),
    margin=dict(l=180),
)
fig.show()
```

## Phase Summary

```{python}
summary_rows = []
for _, grp in df.groupby("phase_num"):
    row = grp.iloc[0]
    exercises = grp["exercise_normalized"].nunique()
    plans = sorted(grp["plan"].unique())
    plan_str = "/".join(plans)
    reps_vals = pd.to_numeric(grp["reps"], errors="coerce").dropna().unique()
    reps_range = (
        f"{int(min(reps_vals))}–{int(max(reps_vals))}"
        if len(reps_vals) > 1
        else str(int(reps_vals[0]))
        if len(reps_vals) == 1
        else "—"
    )
    weeks = int(grp["week"].max())
    summary_rows.append(
        {
            "Phase": row["phase"],
            "Start Date": str(row["phase_date"])[:10] if pd.notna(row["phase_date"]) else "—",
            "Weeks": weeks,
            "Exercises": exercises,
            "Plans": plan_str,
            "Reps": reps_range,
        }
    )

summary = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows)
show(summary, paging=False, searching=False)
```

---

::: {.text-center .text-muted style="font-size: 0.85em; padding: 1em 0;"}
Built with [Claude Code](https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-code-in-action) | [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/valentinklotzbuecher/statera_vk)
:::
